snippets.title: MLflow: run ID, tags, and compare params
snippets.code:
import mlflow

with mlflow.start_run(run_name="sentiment-baseline") as run:
    run_id = run.info.run_id
    mlflow.set_tag("team", "nlp")
    mlflow.set_tag("stage", "candidate")
    mlflow.log_param("learning_rate", 1e-3)
    mlflow.log_metric("val_f1", 0.842)
    print("experiment_id:", run_id)

# Later: fetch and compare two runs by ID
client = mlflow.tracking.MlflowClient()
for rid in ["abc123", "def456"]:
    r = client.get_run(rid)
    print(rid, r.data.params, r.data.metrics)
snippets.explanation: Each `start_run` block gets a unique `run_id`. Tags group runs; params and metrics enable programmatic comparison by ID.

snippets.title: MLflow model registry: register from a run
snippets.code:
import mlflow
from mlflow.models import infer_signature

# Assume run_id and model already logged under that run
run_id = "7b2e10d4a1b24c8fa2c9e0d3f5a67890"
model_uri = f"runs:/{run_id}/model"

result = mlflow.register_model(
    model_uri=model_uri,
    name="fraud-detector",
)
print("registered version:", result.version)
print("source run:", result.run_id)
snippets.explanation: `register_model` creates a named version in the registry with lineage to the source run ID and artifact path.

snippets.title: W&B: run ID, tags, and run comparison table
snippets.code:
import wandb

run = wandb.init(project="churn-model", name="xgb-v3", tags=["baseline", "xgb"])
run_id = run.id
wandb.config.update({"max_depth": 6, "seed": 42})
wandb.log({"val_auc": 0.91})
run.finish()

# In the W&B UI: filter runs with tag "baseline" and sort by val_auc
# Or use the API to list runs and build a comparison dataframe
snippets.explanation: W&B assigns each run an `id`. Tags and logged config/metrics support filtering and side-by-side comparison in the UI or API.
